title: Product Manager
description:
Overview:

SPOTIO was founded in 2014 and is a privately held company based in Addison, Texas. We are known as a leading field sales engagement platform that helps thousands of sales professionals worldwide increase activity, grow their pipeline, provide visibility, and crush sales goals. SPOTIO is consistently ranked a Leader in the G2 Grid for Field Sales and named one of Dallas-Fort Worth's Best Places to Work by the Dallas Business Journal in 2025 and five times overall.

The Role

We’re hiring a Product Manager with 3+ years of experience to own product strategy and execution across our web app and native mobile apps (iOS and Android). You’ll partner closely with Design, Engineering, AI/Data, Sales, and Customer Success to turn complex field-sales workflows and AI capabilities into shippable, measurable product outcomes.

This is a high-impact role on a product-led team. Your decisions will directly influence how thousands of sales professionals plan their day, capture customer information, and act on AI-driven insights in the field.

What You’ll Do
Own end-to-end product delivery for features across SPOTIO’s web platform and mobile apps — from discovery and problem definition through launch, iteration, and impact measurement.
Drive AI-first product thinking by identifying where ML, predictive insights, and generative AI can meaningfully improve the rep and manager experience — and shaping how those capabilities show up in the product.
Define and prioritize the roadmap for your area, balancing customer needs, business goals, technical investment, and AI experimentation against clear, measurable outcomes.
Write crisp specs and PRDs that articulate the problem, user, success metrics, scope, and edge cases — and keep them living documents as you learn.
Partner deeply with Design and Engineering throughout discovery, design, build, and launch — making fast, well-reasoned trade-offs as new information surfaces.
Talk to customers constantly — sales reps, managers, and admins — through interviews, ride-alongs, support tickets, and product analytics to ground every decision in real behavior.
Define and track success metrics using product analytics, A/B tests, and qualitative feedback; communicate results clearly to the company.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, CS, and Support on launches, enablement, and feedback loops.

Requirements

What We’re Looking For
3+ years of product management experience at a SaaS company, ideally B2B and/or B2C, with shipped work across both web and mobile.
A strong track record of outcomes — you can point to specific products or features you’ve owned, the decisions you made, and the measurable impact you drove.
Native mobile product fluency with a clear understanding of the differences between designing and shipping for iOS, Android, and responsive web.
Experience shipping AI-driven features such as recommendations, predictive scoring, generative interfaces, smart automation, or LLM-powered workflows — and a point of view on how to evaluate and improve them.
Leveraging Claude to work as your assistant
Comfort with data — SQL, product analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar), and experimentation — to inform decisions and measure success.
Strong product sense and user empathy — you sweat the details, push for simplicity, and balance long-term vision with shipping value every sprint.
Excellent written and verbal communication with the ability to align engineers, designers, executives, and customers around a shared plan.
A collaborative, pragmatic approach — you partner well with Design and Engineering, give and receive feedback gracefully, and lead through influence, not authority.
Nice to Have
Experience in outside sales, field service, CRM, or other complex B2B workflow tools.
Familiarity with LLM-based product patterns (chat, agents, copilots, in-context assistance) and how to evaluate them in production.
Background working with offline-capable or location-aware mobile apps.
Technical background — CS degree, engineering experience, or comfort reading code and reasoning about system trade-offs.
Experience growing a product from early stage through scale.

Benefits

401K with 3% company match
Medical Insurance
Dental Insurance
Vision Insurance
9 paid holidays
3 weeks of paid time off
Voluntary benefits (pet insurance, legal, accident)
24/7 EAP - Free Professional Counseling Services

SPOTIO Work Environment

This position is hybrid. We work in the office twice a week on Tuesday and Wednesday.

SPOTIO Values

Solve For the Customer - know their goals, business, and how we help customers achieve a 10x ROI
Play to Win - we lead the way to victory through ownership, urgency, and competitive mindset
Make it Great - be the best version of yourself for your family, team, customer and company
We Know Where We Stand - open, honest, and timely feedback with clear visibility to the metrics that matter
Relationships Matter - collaborate with team members, customers, and

title: Product Manager (Banking-as-a-Service) - Relocation to Cyprus
description:
The role is about:

We are looking for a senior, commercially sharp Product Manager to lead the growth and expansion of our Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform — a critical initiative that will unlock new revenue streams and scale payabl.'s banking capabilities for external partners.

This is not a feature-factory product role. You will own a mission-critical platform that enables fintech companies, brands, and financial institutions to embed banking services into their products without building their own core banking systems. Your focus will be on partner enablement, API-driven product strategy, and scalable multi-tenant architecture.

You will define product direction for our BaaS ecosystem, set partner-focused priorities, and ensure the platform evolves in line with our North Star: sustainable growth in active BaaS partners and transaction volumes through the BaaS channel.

You will work at the intersection of Product, Engineering, and Partner Success — collaborating closely with developers, delivery managers, business stakeholders, and external partners to ensure we build a scalable, technically sound, and commercially viable platform that delivers real value.

If you enjoy owning complex, multi-tenant platforms, thinking in partnership and ecosystem terms, and driving impact through disciplined product strategy and partner success — this role is for you.

Reporting Line: CPO

Location: Cyprus — Onsite (employment contract with relocation support)

What will you do:

Own and evolve the BaaS platform roadmap (3, 6, and 12-month horizons) with a focus on partner adoption, integration ease, and revenue growth
Define product direction aligned with the company North Star (30% growth in active BaaS partners)
Build and scale the BaaS API and partner ecosystem to support partner growth and transaction volumes
Design and own the partner onboarding experience — from API documentation to SDKs, sandbox environments, and integration support
Identify technical bottlenecks in partner integration flows and reduce friction (time-to-integrate, complexity, support burden)
Work closely with Engineering to understand multi-tenant architecture, scaling constraints, and integration capabilities
Translate partner feedback and market opportunities into structured product initiatives and roadmap priorities
Drive discovery, API documentation, and architectural alignment with partner success teams
Ensure sustainable, scalable solutions that generate revenue and improve partner lifetime value
Be accountable for partner satisfaction metrics, integration success rates, and BaaS channel revenue
Collaborate closely with Delivery Manager and Engineering Lead to ensure roadmap execution and SLA compliance
Align internal stakeholders (Sales, Compliance, Operations, Finance) on partner-facing capabilities and commercial models
Organize monthly partner reviews and internal product reviews to communicate direction, partner feedback, and decisions
Evaluate third-party integrations and partnerships when needed (payment processors, KYC/AML providers, etc.)
Own the expansion of API-first banking capabilities (accounts, cards, payments, compliance tools) for partner consumption
Support partner success through technical enablement, documentation, and community engagement

What we need:

5+ years of experience as a Product Manager or Product Owner in BaaS, API platforms, or fintech environments
Strong experience with Banking-as-a-Service or API-first financial platforms — understanding of multi-tenant architecture, partner ecosystems, and integration complexity
Technical depth in system architecture, APIs, integrations, and scalability — ability to discuss technical trade-offs and constraints with engineers
Experience managing partner-facing products or platforms — understanding of partner lifecycle, onboarding, and success metrics
Strong data-driven mindset and analytical skills — ability to measure partner adoption, integration success, and revenue impact
Experience building and managing structured roadmaps aligned to business outcomes and partner needs
Experience working in Agile / Scrum environments and coordinating cross-functional delivery
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills — ability to align internal teams, partners, and executives on priorities and trade-offs
Understanding of compliance and regulatory requirements in fintech/banking environments (KYC, AML, licensing, data residency)

Nice to have:

Engineering background or early technical career experience — ability to code or deep technical familiarity
Experience scaling high-transaction platforms (millions of transactions, multi-region, multi-currency)
Experience in fintech scale-ups or fast-growing environments
Exposure to open banking / PSD2 / API-first banking models
Experience with payment rails (SEPA, SWIFT, card processing, instant payments)
Prior experience launching or scaling a BaaS platform from 0→1 or 1→10 partners

title: Product Manager Júnior | RD Station (Remoto) (Remoto, Brasil)
Product Manager, Mobile
description:
The world of digital assets is accelerating in speed, magnitude, and complexity, opening the door to new ways for leveraging the blockchain. Fireblocks’ platform and network provide the simplest and most secure way for companies to work with digital assets and it trusted by some of the largest financial institutions, banks, globally-recognized brands, and Web3 companies in the world, including BNY Mellon, BNP Paribas, ANZ Bank, Revolut, and thousands more. 

Founded in 2018, Fireblocks is a SaaS platform that helps companies create innovative products on the blockchain and manage day-to-day crypto operations. We enable financial institutions to securely build, run, and scale digital asset operations through the Fireblocks Network and MPC-based wallet infrastructure.
 
About the role
Fireblocks' mobile app is a critical component in the day-to-day financial operations of the world's leading financial institutions.
As the Mobile PM, you'll own the full product domain: driving the roadmap, collaborating closely with engineering and design, and ensuring every feature ships with precision and measurable impact. But this is more than a customer-facing product - it's also a foundational platform that other product teams across Fireblocks rely on to deliver their features to mobile users. You'll own both dimensions: driving the customer experience outbound while advancing the infrastructure that makes it possible. As the world moves toward agentic, AI-native financial operations, this team is building exactly that. You define what gets built and why.

 What you'll own

The mobile product end-to-end - roadmap, strategy, and delivery across iOS and Android, with full accountability for outcomes.
The customer experience - deeply understanding how institutional users interact with their financial operations on mobile, and continuously raising the bar.
The mobile platform - working with product teams across Fireblocks to understand their needs and ensuring mobile can support their features effectively.
The AI and intelligence layer on mobile - defining how smart, proactive capabilities get surfaced to users in a way that adds real value.
What the work actually looks like
Spending significant time with customers and data - forming a sharp, evidence-based view of what to build next and why.
Making hard prioritization calls - balancing customer-facing improvements, platform needs from internal teams, and long-term product investments.
Working across teams - product, engineering, design, security, and customer success - to align on scope and get things shipped.
Owning the full arc of a feature - from problem definition and spec to launch, adoption, and iteration.
What you bring
4+ years in product management; experience owning a mobile product domain is a strong plus.
Experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise software - you understand multi-stakeholder users and complex workflows.
Strong data fluency - you write queries, build your own analysis, and don't wait for a data team to tell you what's happening.
Technical enough to have real conversations with engineers - you know what "hard" looks like and write specs that leave no room for ambiguity.
Experience with AI-powered features is a strong plus - especially mobile or workflow-embedded AI.
Nice to have
Background in fintech, digital assets, crypto, or financial infrastructure.
Experience with MPC wallets, custody platforms, or transaction approval flows.
Experience working with enterprise customers in high-security, high-stakes environments.

Fireblocks' mission is to enable every business to easily and securely access digital assets and cryptocurrencies. In order to do that, we strongly believe our workforce should be as diverse as our clients, and this is why we embrace diversity and inclusion in all its forms.
